children.label: A
children.text: certification or licensure as elementary or secondary school teachers; or

children.label: B
children.text: the credentials necessary to serve as teachers’ aides; and

children.label: A
children.text: are receiving grants under title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.) as a result of having within their jurisdictions concentrations of children from low-income families; and20 U.S.C. 6301

children.label: B
children.text: are also experiencing a shortage of teachers or teachers’ aides.

children.label: A
children.text: during the five-year period beginning , is terminated from such employment as a result of reductions in defense spending or the closure or realignment of a military installation, as determined by the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of Energy, as the case may be;October 1, 1992

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an employee applying for assistance for placement as an elementary or secondary school teacher, a baccalaureate or advanced degree from an accredited institution of higher education; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an employee applying for assistance for placement as a teacher’s aide in an elementary or secondary school, an associate, baccalaureate, or advanced degree from an accredited institution of higher education or a junior or community college; and

children.label: C
children.text: satisfies such other criteria for selection as the Secretary of Defense may prescribe.

The Secretary of Defense may accept an application from a civilian employee referred to in paragraph (1) who was terminated during the period beginning on , and ending on , if the employee otherwise satisfies the eligibility criteria specified in that paragraph.October 1, 1990October 1, 1992

Selection of civilian employees to participate in the placement program shall be made on the basis of applications submitted to the Secretary of Defense after the employees receive a notice of termination. An application shall be filed within such time, in such form, and contain such information as the Secretary of Defense may require.

children.label: A
children.text: have educational, military, or employment experience in science, mathematics, or engineering and agree to seek employment as science, mathematics, or engineering teachers in elementary or secondary schools; or

children.label: B
children.text: have educational, military, or employment experience in another subject area identified by the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Education, as important for national educational objectives and agree to seek employment in that subject area in elementary or secondary schools.

The Secretary of Defense may not select a civilian employee to participate in the program unless the Secretary has sufficient appropriations for the placement program available at the time of the selection to satisfy the obligations to be incurred by the United States under the program with respect to that member.

to obtain, within such time as the Secretary may require, certification or licensure as an elementary or secondary school teacher or the necessary credentials to serve as a teacher’s aide in an elementary or secondary school; and

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an employee selected for assistance for placement as a teacher, an offer of full-time employment as an elementary or secondary school teacher for not less than two school years with a local educational agency identified under section 1151(b)(2) of this title, as in effect on , to begin the school year after obtaining that certification or licensure; or1See References in Text note below.1October 4, 1999

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an employee selected for assistance for placement as a teacher’s aide, an offer of full-time employment as a teacher’s aide in an elementary or secondary school for not less than two school years with a local educational agency identified under section 1151(b)(3) of this title, as in effect on , to begin the school year after obtaining the necessary credentials.1October 4, 1999

children.label: B
children.text: the total costs of the type described in paragraphs (1), (2), (3), (8), and (9) of section 472 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 () incurred by the participant while obtaining teacher certification or licensure or the necessary credentials to serve as a teacher’s aide and employment as an elementary or secondary school teacher or teacher aide.120 U.S.C. 1087ll
A civilian employee selected to participate in the placement program who receives separation pay under shall not be paid a stipend under paragraph (1).section 5597 of title 5

A stipend paid under paragraph (1) shall be taken into account in determining the eligibility of the participant for Federal student financial assistance provided under title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1070

children.heading: .—Placement of Participants as Teachers and Teachers’ Aides
children.label: f .—Placement of Participants as Teachers and Teachers’ Aides
children.text: Subsections (h) through (k) of section 1151 of this title, as in effect on , shall apply with respect to the placement program authorized by this section.1October 4, 1999
